Safety Mar 30, 2026

Inside The Boeing 737 MAX's Flight Control System

How pilots fly the Boeing 737 MAX.

Understanding the 737 MAX's flight control architecture is essential for pilots to recognize when automated systems are intervening and maintain situational awareness during critical phases.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

ESA launches first Celeste satellites to test complementary LEO navigation layer

MILAN — The European Space Agency has launched the first two satellites of the Celeste in-orbit demonstration mission from New Zealand aboard a Rocket Lab Electron launcher on March 28. The two satellites were launched at 10:14 CET and separated from the launcher about an hour later into a...

Europe's Celeste LEO constellation provides a GPS-independent navigation backup for aviation, reducing reliance on single-system vulnerabilities in critical positioning infrastructure.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

SpaceX launches Transporter-16 rideshare mission

SpaceX launched the latest in its series of dedicated rideshare missions March 30, delivering more than 100 payloads to sun-synchronous orbit.

SpaceX's rideshare model demonstrates how dedicated multi-payload launches reduce costs for smaller satellite operators, creating new market opportunities in the commercial launch sector.

See SpaceNews →

General Aviation Mar 30, 2026

Picture of the Day: March 30, 2026

Formation flying with a friend's Subaru-powered home built.

Subaru automotive engines continue enabling cost-effective homebuilt aircraft construction for recreational aviators seeking affordable formation flying experiences.

Military Mar 30, 2026

5 Largest Aircraft Fleets Operated By The US Air Force

From F-16 fighters to KC-135 tankers, these five aircraft types make up the largest fleets in the US Air Force in 2026.

The USAF's fleet composition reveals aging platforms like the KC-135 (built 1956) still dominate tanker operations, highlighting modernization investment gaps amid peer-competitor threats.

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Selecting Risk Controls and Accepting Residual Risk

Where Safety Risk Management moves from analysis to action — how organizations develop controls under §5.55(c)–(d).

Organizations must document their rationale for accepting residual risk after controls are implemented, creating an auditable decision trail for regulatory compliance.

eVTOL Mar 30, 2026

Merlin Labs sees incremental path to pilot-free passenger flights

US start-up Merlin Labs is eyeing civil applications for its AI-powered, defense-grade Merlin Pilot autonomous flight control system and says there is “no hard ceiling” for the size of aircraft its technology could eventually be installed on. Describing fully uncrewed civil flight as “a very long...

Merlin Labs' defense-grade autonomous system scalability to larger aircraft signals that passenger drone certification timelines may be driven by regulatory readiness rather than technical limitations.

See Runway Girl Network →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Spain Bars U.S. Iran-Related Military Flights From Its Airspace

Madrid expands earlier restrictions on base access for U.S. military flights.

Spain's escalating airspace restrictions on U.S. military operations signal growing European political constraints on NATO ally activities, potentially complicating transatlantic military logistics and operational flexibility.

See AVweb →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

China’s Kinetica-2 rocket debuts successfully, sending prototype cargo spacecraft to orbit

Chinese firm CAS Space successfully reached orbit with the first launch of its Kinetica-2 launch vehicle Monday, with a prototype cargo spacecraft among the payloads.

China's emerging commercial space sector now demonstrates reusable rocket capability, potentially disrupting global launch market pricing and capacity.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Airbus Faces Persistent Engine Shortages Threatening Future Aircraft Deliveries

The European planemaker is gearing up for its AGM next month.

Engine supply constraints are becoming a structural bottleneck that could force Airbus to revise production ramp timelines regardless of strong order books.

Safety Mar 30, 2026

The Evolution of Aviation SMS

From the Wright brothers to the 2024 FAA rule — how aviation safety evolved from reactive investigation to proactive risk management.

Modern SMS transforms aviation from investigating accidents after they occur to systematically identifying and mitigating risks before they cause incidents.

Regulatory Mar 30, 2026

Traffic Resumes After Potomac TRACON Evacuation

Ground stop affected Washington-area airports Friday for the second time in two weeks.

Repeated evacuation incidents at Potomac TRACON within two weeks suggest systemic facility or procedural issues requiring urgent FAA investigation to prevent future operational disruptions.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Press Release: The New Terminal One at JFK selects PRA services vendor

The New Terminal One at JFK has selected Wilson James, a leading provider of Passengers Requiring Assistance (PRA) services, as its dedicated partner to design and operate a comprehensive assistance program for passengers who require additional support when traveling through the new...

Terminal One's PRA partnership signals that major airport modernization projects now prioritize accessibility infrastructure as a core operational component rather than an afterthought.

See Runway Girl Network →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Emirates Pulls The Airbus A380 From 15 Major Routes: Here's Why

The continuing war in Iran has led to a drastic reduction in Emirates' A380 departures from Dubai, with plans subject to rapid change.

Geopolitical instability is forcing airlines to reconfigure their largest aircraft deployments, reducing hub capacity and requiring rapid route reassessments.

See Simple Flying →

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Bomb Threat Holds Up Frontier Flight in Atlanta

FBI says there is no continuing threat after passenger’s verbal bomb-threat.

Unverified bomb threats can trigger costly operational disruptions and security responses even when quickly determined to be non-credible by authorities.

Safety Mar 30, 2026

Flying in the Unforgiving Wire Environment (The Rotorcraft Collective)

Many pilots mistakenly believe they will have sufficient reaction time if they encounter power lines and other wires. Yet wire strikes have been among the top three causes of fatal helicopter...

Wire strike fatalities persist because pilots systematically overestimate their ability to detect and avoid obstacles at operational altitudes and speeds.

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

180 Liters Of Water: The Unusual Test Powering The New Airbus A350F

Another key milestone for the forthcoming widebody freighter.

Airbus's water ingestion testing for the A350F validates engine durability in extreme moisture conditions, critical for cargo operations in diverse global climates.

Airbus’ uncrewed Bird of Prey interceptor autonomously engages kamikaze drone with Frankenburg missile

The Airbus ‘Bird of Prey’ interceptor drone successfully completed its first demonstration flight at a...

Airbus demonstrated autonomous air-to-air combat capability, marking a significant milestone in unmanned weapons systems integration and AI-driven tactical decision-making.

Military Mar 30, 2026

Spain Continues to Deny Airspace to U.S. Aircraft Involved with Operation Epic Fury

Spanish newspaper El País reported further on earlier news regarding Spain’s position on U.S. military action against Iran, with the country’s Prime Minister confirming all related flight plans have been rejected. After a mass exodus of U.S. Air Force (USAF) aerial refueling aircraft from Spanish...

Spain's airspace denial forces USAF to reroute refueling operations, significantly extending mission ranges and logistics complexity for any Iran-related contingency operations.

See The Aviationist →

Remote BVLOS operations across international borders demonstrate how drone services are scaling beyond single-site deployments to support major infrastructure projects across Europe.

Military Mar 30, 2026

U.S. Navy Launches Competition for T-45’s Replacement with Final RFP

The U.S. Navy has released the final Request for Proposals for the Undergraduate Jet Training System which will replace the T-45 Goshawk, confirming an accelerated schedule with a focus on costs. The U.S. Navy has released on Mar. 26, 2026, the final Request for Proposals (RFP) for the...

The Navy's accelerated T-45 replacement timeline signals increased competition among defense contractors and potential disruption to current training aircraft supply chains.

See The Aviationist →

Industry Mar 30, 2026

Towards Environmentally Sustainable Aviation: A Review on Operational Optimization

Growing air traffic is straining airports and airspace while raising environmental concerns, and demand is only expected to increase. This paper reviews how advanced technologies and methods can expand capacity and improve efficiency across airline, airport, and flight operations. It also highlights emerging trends and gaps for future research in sustainable aviation.

Operational optimization across all aviation sectors offers immediate capacity gains without infrastructure expansion, making efficiency improvements a critical lever for meeting environmental targets amid forecasted traffic growth.

See MDPI →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Why Boeing Built The 777X With A New & Elegant Cabin Architecture

Modern, streamlined and elegant, the new cabin architecture could change the passenger experience forever.

The 777X's modular cabin design enables airlines to reconfigure interior layouts mid-lifecycle, reducing costly aircraft retirements and extending revenue-generating service life.

General Aviation Mar 30, 2026

Sonex Announces Immediate Closure

Owner Mark Schaible cites sales decline, lender pressure and cash-flow constraints in video message.

Sonex's collapse signals vulnerability in the recreational aircraft market when production volumes cannot sustain fixed costs amid tightening credit conditions.

Military Mar 30, 2026

Before The F-35 Vs. After: How Air Combat Changed

Examining a paradigm shift in air warfare.

The F-35's integration of sensor fusion and network-centric warfare fundamentally shifted air combat from platform-centric dogfighting to information-dominant stand-off engagement.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

The race to build orbital data centers is missing its biggest variable: power

Here’s the version of the orbital data center story you keep reading: Elon Musk says space will be the cheapest place to run AI within 36 months. LoneStar announces plans for a lunar data center. NVIDIA’s Vera Rubin Space-1 makes headlines. These are real announcements, and they all have one thing...

Orbital data center ventures lack realistic power infrastructure assessments, creating a critical gap between ambitious timelines and engineering feasibility that could reshape space commerce economics.

See SpaceNews →

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

With Voyager’s help, Icarus Robotics to test free-flyer on ISS

SAN FRANCISCO – Voyager Technologies announced plans March 30 to help Icarus Robotics test a free-flying platform, called Joyride, on the International Space Station. Under the agreement, Voyager will handle payload integration, safety certification, coordination of a 2027 launch, on-orbit...

Commercial space logistics providers are enabling rapid ISS technology demonstrations, with Voyager handling full integration and certification to accelerate orbital platform validation timelines.

See SpaceNews →

Military Mar 30, 2026

Trump Says F-35 Sale to Saudi Arabia Has Been Approved

For the second time, President Trump says that the U.S. agreed to sell F-35s to Saudi Arabia as relations between the two countries keep improving. For the second time since late last year, reports of U.S. acquiescence for the sale of F-35A Lightning II stealth fighters to the Kingdom of Saudi...

F-35 sales to Saudi Arabia signal deepening U.S.-Saudi defense partnership and represent a strategic shift in Middle East military capabilities.

See The Aviationist →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Delta VIP Travel Perks Suspended For Members Of Congress Amid Budget Dispute

Extremely long wait times to get through security at US airports has been commonplace in recent weeks as a partial government shutdown has forced airport security workers to work without pay until their portion of the budget can be passed by politicians. As a result, Delta Air Lines said on...

Delta's suspension of congressional perks signals airlines are willing to leverage customer service as political pressure during budget disputes, potentially setting precedent for industry action.

See Simple Flying →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Discover the intriguing backstory behind Virgin's new longest service...

Ultra-long-haul routes require reconfigured cabins and crew scheduling innovations that challenge traditional aircraft utilization models for efficiency.

Aerospace Mar 30, 2026

Starcloud achieves unicorn status with $170 million raise for orbital data centers

Starcloud has raised $170 million to accelerate development of its next-generation spacecraft, reaching a $1.1 billion valuation as it awaits permission to deploy an 88,000-strong orbital data center network.

Orbital data center infrastructure represents a new revenue frontier for space tech, requiring regulatory approval for large-scale deployment that could transform satellite constellation economics.

See SpaceNews →

Commercial Mar 30, 2026

Best Transpacific Airlines (Premium Comfort Revealed)

For years, flying across the Pacific Ocean meant preparing for one of the most exhausting journeys in commercial aviation. A trip between the US West Coast and East Asia could easily stretch beyond ten hours, and passengers often judged the experience by how well they could simply survive the...

Airlines competing on transpacific routes are differentiating through premium cabin amenities and passenger comfort features rather than just schedule, recognizing fatigue mitigation as a competitive advantage.
